human brain.description: The brain is the central organ of the nervous system, containing approximately 86 billion neurons that control all body functions.
key_facts: Contains approximately 86 billion neurons
Consumes about 20% of the body's total energy and oxygen
Consists of about 75% water
Information travels at speeds of up to 268 miles per hour
human brain.full_text: The human brain is the central organ of the human nervous system. It is the largest brain of all vertebrates relative to body size and is the most complex brain.

human heart.description: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood throughout the circulatory system, beating about 100,000 times per day.
key_facts: Beats approximately 100,000 times per day
Pumps about 2,000 gallons of blood daily
Has four chambers: two atria and two ventricles
About the size of a closed fist
human heart.full_text: The heart is a muscular organ in most animals. This organ pumps blood through the blood vessels of the circulatory system.

nervous system.description: The nervous system is a network of nerve cells that transmit signals throughout the body, divided into central and peripheral systems.
key_facts: Divided into central and peripheral nervous systems
Transmits signals at speeds up to 120 meters per second
Contains both sensory and motor neurons
Coordinates all body functions and responses
nervous system.full_text: The nervous system is the part of an animal's body that coordinates its actions by transmitting signals to and from different parts of its body.

skeleton.description: The skeleton is the framework of bones that supports the body and protects organs, consisting of 206 bones in adults.
key_facts: Adults have 206 bones
Makes up about 15% of total body weight
Produces red and white blood cells
Provides attachment points for muscles
skeleton.full_text: The skeleton is the structural basis that supports the human body. It is a living tissue that constantly breaks down and regenerates.

skull.description: The skull is the bone structure of the head that protects the brain and supports facial features, containing 22 bones.
key_facts: Contains 22 bones
Protects the brain from injury
Supports and shapes the face
Contains cavities for the eyes, nose, and ears
skull.full_text: The skull is a bone structure in the head that forms the framework for the face and protects the brain.

explanation.description: "The Explanation offers clear, concise breakdowns of complex concepts, making learning accessible and engaging."

neuron.description: The Neuron is a specialized cell that processes and transmits information within the nervous system via electrical and chemical signals.

definition of meaning.description: "The Definition of Meaning explores how words and symbols convey meaning within languages."

cat.description: The Cat is a carnivorous mammal known for its agility and independent nature. It has retractable claws, sharp teeth, and a keen sense of sight and hearing. Domestic cats are popular pets worldwide.
